title: Prime and composite numbers
description: Practice identifying prime and composite numbers.
topic_path: Get ready for equations, expressions, and inequalities

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following describes the numbers shaded on the hundred chart?**

![hundred chart with 7, 27, 47, and 67 shaded](web+graphie:${‚ė£ LOCALPATH}/images/6e3b35f964a6c0dd62d4929e696c9375b0c1a554)

[[‚ėÉ radio 1]]
hints:
A prime number is a number that has exactly two factors (including $1$ and itself).

A composite number has more than two factors.

The factors of $27$ are $1$, $3$, $9$, and $27$.

The number $27$ has more than two factors, so it is composite.

The numbers $7$, $47$, and $67$ each have exactly two factors, $1$ and itself.

[[‚ėÉ explanation 1]]
Some shaded numbers are prime and some are composite.

choices.content: The shaded numbers are all prime.
The shaded numbers are all composite.
Some shaded numbers are prime and some are composite.

assessment_items.question_content:
All of the multiples of $3$ on the hundred chart are shaded.

**Which of the following describes the numbers shaded on the hundred chart?**

![hundred chart with the multiples of 3 from 3 to 99 shaded](web+graphie:${‚ė£ LOCALPATH}/images/0d7888621a0dbf86a2d635dd9ece47852447687f)

[[‚ėÉ radio 1]]
hints:
A prime number is a number that has exactly two factors (including $1$ and itself).

A composite number has more than two factors.

The number $3$ has exactly two factors, $1$ and itself.

[[‚ėÉ explanation 1]]

The rest of the numbers are composite, because each shaded number has at least three factors: 

* $1$
* $3$
* itself
Some shaded numbers are prime and some are composite.

choices.content: The shaded numbers are all prime.
The shaded numbers are all composite.
Some shaded numbers are prime and some are composite.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following describes the numbers shaded on the hundred chart?**

![hundred chart with 22, 33, 44, and 55 shaded](web+graphie:${‚ė£ LOCALPATH}/images/4fac939e412163904d3202e91114ef825009b506)

[[‚ėÉ radio 1]]
hints:
A prime number is a number that has exactly two factors (including $1$ and itself).

A composite number has more than two factors.
The numbers are all multiples of $11$ and greater than $11$.

The numbers are composite, because each shaded number has at least three factors: 

* $1$
* $11$
* itself
The shaded numbers are all composite.

choices.content: The shaded numbers are all prime.
The shaded numbers are all composite.
Some shaded numbers are prime and some are composite.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following describes the numbers shaded on the hundred chart?**

![hundred chart with 31, 43, 59, and 89 shaded](web+graphie:${‚ė£ LOCALPATH}/images/8eaf4406df4f1dd5f5fddc708c928c4cb5a43156)

[[‚ėÉ radio 1]]
hints:
A prime number is a number that has exactly two factors (including $1$ and itself).

A composite number has more than two factors.

The number $31$ has exactly two factors, $1$ and itself.

[[‚ėÉ explanation 1]]

The numbers $43$, $59$, and $89$ also each have exactly two factors, $1$ and itself.

All the numbers are prime.
The shaded numbers are all prime.

choices.content: The shaded numbers are all prime.
The shaded numbers are all composite.
Some shaded numbers are prime and some are composite.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jon tried to shade only composite numbers on the hundred chart.

However, Jon made a mistake.

[[‚ėÉ image 1]]

**What mistake did Jon make?**

[[‚ėÉ radio 2]]
hints:
A prime number is a number that has exactly two factors (including $1$ and itself).

A composite number has more than two factors.

The factors of $51$ are $1$, $3$, $17$, and $51$.

The number $51$ has more than two factors, so it is composite.

The number $61$ has exactly two factors, $1$ and itself.

[[‚ėÉ explanation 1]]

The factors of $84$ are $1$, $2$, $3$, $4$, $6$, $7$, $12$, $14$, $21$, $28$, $42$ and $84$.

The number $84$ has more than two factors, so it is composite.
$61$ is prime, not composite, so Jon made a mistake.

options.alt: A hundred chart with 51, 61, and 84 shaded.

choices.content: $51$ is prime, not composite.
$61$ is prime, not composite.
$84$ is prime, not composite.

assessment_items.question_content:
Camila tried to shade only composite numbers on the hundred chart.

However, Camila made a mistake.

![hundred chart with 29, 39, and 49 shaded](web+graphie:${‚ė£ LOCALPATH}/images/3ed23b0955ad27b7d7cf34dadbbe863873b74784)

**What mistake did Camila make?**

[[‚ėÉ radio 2]]
hints:
A prime number is a number that has exactly two factors (including $1$ and itself).

A composite number has more than two factors.

The number $29$ has exactly two factors, $1$ and itself.

[[‚ėÉ explanation 1]]
$29$ is prime, not composite, so Camila made a mistake.

choices.content: $29$ is prime, not composite.
$39$ is prime, not composite.
$49$ is prime, not composite.

assessment_items.question_content:
Eliott tried to shade only prime numbers on the hundred chart.

However, Elliot made a mistake.

![hundred chart with 13, 23, and 33 shaded](web+graphie:${‚ė£ LOCALPATH}/images/da0ecbb0659f42f0237fbf10bee3b50f5b9146bf)

**What mistake did Eliott make?**

[[‚ėÉ radio 2]]
hints:
A prime number is a number that has exactly two factors (including $1$ and itself).

A composite number has more than two factors.

The numbers $13$ and $23$ each have exactly two factors, $1$ and itself.

[[‚ėÉ explanation 1]]

The factors of $33$ are $1$, $3$, $11$, and $33$.

The number $33$ has more than two factors, so it is composite.
$33$ is composite, not prime, so Eliott made a mistake.

choices.content: $13$ is composite, not prime.
$23$ is composite, not prime.
$33$ is composite, not prime.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jing tried to shade only prime numbers on the hundred chart.

However, Jing made a mistake.

![hundred chart with 71, 73, and 75 shaded](web+graphie:${‚ė£ LOCALPATH}/images/8124334a4f598edc621aaf2b908feab21dd292cd)

**What mistake did Jing make?**

[[‚ėÉ radio 2]]
hints:
A prime number is a number that has exactly two factors (including $1$ and itself).

A composite number has more than two factors.

The numbers $71$ and $73$ each have exactly two factors, $1$ and itself.

[[‚ėÉ explanation 1]]

The factors of $75$ are $1$, $3$, $5$, $15$, $25$, and $75$.

The number $75$ has more than two factors, so it is composite.
$75$ is composite, not prime, so Jing made a mistake.

choices.content: $71$ is composite, not prime.
$73$ is composite, not prime.
$75$ is composite, not prime.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Is each number prime or composite?**

[[‚ėÉ categorizer 1]]
hints:
A prime number is a number that has exactly two factors (including $1$ and itself).

A composite number has more than two factors.

The number $2$ has exactly two factors, $1$ and itself.

[[‚ėÉ explanation 1]]

The factors of $39$ are $1$, $3$, $13$, and $39$.

The number $39$ has more than two factors, so it is composite.

The factors of $93$ are $1$, $3$, $31$, and $93$.

The number $93$ has more than two factors, so it is composite.
[[‚ėÉ categorizer 2]]

assessment_items.question_content:
**Is each number prime or composite?**

[[‚ėÉ categorizer 1]]
hints:
A prime number is a number that has exactly two factors (including $1$ and itself).

A composite number has more than two factors, with at least one factor pair that does not include itself.

The factors of $10$ are $1$, $2$, $5$, and $10$.

The number $10$ has more than two factors, so it is composite.

The number $41$ has exactly two factors, $1$ and itself.

[[‚ėÉ explanation 1]]

The factors of $86$ are $1$, $2$, $43$, and $86$.

The number $86$ has more than two factors, so it is composite.
[[‚ėÉ categorizer 3]]

assessment_items.question_content:
**Is each number prime or composite?**

[[‚ėÉ categorizer 1]]
hints:
A prime number is a number that has exactly two factors (including $1$ and itself).

A composite number has more than two factors.

The number $5$ has exactly two factors, $1$ and itself.

[[‚ėÉ explanation 1]]

The factors of $9$ are $1$, $3$, and $9$.

The number $9$ has more than two factors, so it is composite.

The factors of $57$ are $1$, $3$, $19$, and $57$.

The number $57$ has more than two factors, so it is composite.
[[‚ėÉ categorizer 4]]

assessment_items.question_content:
**Is each number prime or composite?**

[[‚ėÉ categorizer 1]]
hints:
A prime number is a number that has exactly two factors (including $1$ and itself).

A composite number has more than two factors.

The number $19$ has exactly two factors, $1$ and itself.
